        have h₇ : (⊥ : PersistenceSubmodule M) ≠ ⊤ := by
          intro h₈
          have h₉ := h₈
          simp [PersistenceSubmodule.ext_iff] at h₉
          <;>
          (try {

    have h₃ : iSupIndep (fun p : I => (p.val c : Submodule K (M.obj c)) : I → Submodule K (M.obj c)) := by
      classical
      -- Use the fact that the set is iSupIndep in the lattice of submodules to deduce the pointwise condition
      have h₄ : iSupIndep (fun p : I => (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) : I → (PersistenceSubmodule M)) := h₂
      simp_all [iSupIndep, sSupIndep, Set.range]
      <;>
      (try {
        aesop
      }) <;>
      (try {
        tauto
      })
    -- Use the iSupIndep condition to prove the statement
    simpa [iSup] using h₃
  · -- Prove that the supremum of the submodules is the entire module
    have h := pointwise_sSup_eq_top I c
    simpa using h

intro p
      rfl
    -- Convert sSupIndep to the required form
    convert Submodule.isIndependent_of_sSupIndep (by
      exact fun (y₁ y₂ : SetLike.coe I) _ => by
        -- Ensure that the order on the carrier set is respected
        have h₃ : y₁ = y₂ := by
          -- Since the carrier is a set, the order is fixed
          cases y₁; cases y₂ <;> simp_all [SetLike.ext_iff]
          <;> aesop
        rw [h₃]
    ) h₁ using 1
    <;> simp_all [Submodule.isIndependent_pairwise]
    <;> aesop
  · -- Prove the iSup of the components is the whole space
    have h₁ : ⨆ (y : I), (y : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = ⊤ := by
      have h₂ : ⨆ (y : I), (y.val : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = ⊤ := by
        have h₃ : sSup (SetLike.coe I) = ⊤ := I.sSup_eq_top'
        have h₄ : ⨆ (y : I), (y.val : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = ⨆ (p : SetLike.coe I), (p : Submodule K (M.obj c)) := by
          congr
          <;> ext <;> simp [SetLike.ext_iff]
          <;> aesop
        calc
          _ = ⨆ (y : I), (y.val : Submodule K (M.obj c)) := rfl
          _ = ⨆ (p : SetLike.coe I), (p : Submodule K (M.obj c)) := by
            congr
            <;> ext <;> simp [SetLike.ext_iff]
            <;> aesop
          _ = (⨆ p : SetLike.coe I, p : Submodule K (M.obj c)) := by simp [iSup]
          _ = ⊤ := by
            have h₅ : (⨆ p : SetLike.coe I, p : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = ⊤ := by
              have h₆ : sSup (SetLike.coe I) = ⊤ := I.sSup_eq_top'
              calc
                (⨆ p : SetLike.coe I, p : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = (⨆ p : SetLike.coe I, p : Submodule K (M.obj c)) := rfl
                _ = (sSup (SetLike.coe I) : Submodule K (M.obj c)) := by
                  -- Use the fact that the supremum of the carrier set is the supremum of the submodules
                  rw [show (⨆ p : SetLike.coe I, p : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = (sSup (SetLike.coe I) : Submodule K (M.obj c)) by
                    rw [iSup_coe]
                    <;> simp [sSup_coe]
                  ]
                _ = ⊤ := by
                  -- Since the supremum of the carrier set is top, this follows directly
                  have h₇ : (sSup (SetLike.coe I) : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = ⊤ := by
                    simpa using h₆
                  rw [h₇]
            rw [h₅]
      have h₅ : ⨆ (y : I), (y : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = ⨆ (y : I), (y.val : Submodule K (M.obj c)) := by
        congr
        <;> ext <;> simp [SetLike.ext_iff]
        <;> aesop
      calc
        ⨆ (y : I), (y : Submodule K (M.obj c)) = ⨆ (y : I), (y.val : Submodule K (M.obj c)) := h₅
        _ = ⊤ := h₂
    simpa using h₁

exact h₁₂
        exact h₇
      rw [h₆]
    exact h₂

theorem is_internal (M : Type) [AddCommGroup M] [Ring α] [Module α M] (S : Set (Submodule α M)) (hS : sSupIndep S) :

simpa [SetLike.coe_setOf] using h₁
    simpa [Submodule.map_coe, SetLike.coe_setOf] using h₂.image _
  · -- Proof of sSup_eq_top
    have h₃ : ⨆ p : I, (p : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = ⊤ := by
      have h₄ : sSup (SetLike.coe I) = ⊤ := I.sSup_eq_top'
      have h₅ : ⨆ p ∈ (SetLike.coe I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c = ⊤ := by
        simpa [sSup_eq_iSup] using DirectSumDecomposition.pointwise_sSup_eq_top I c

    simpa [Submodule.map_coe, SetLike.coe_setOf] using h₃
sorry

-- Prove the supremum part
  · have h₂ : ⨆ (p : I), (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = ⊤ := by
      have h₃ : ⨆ (p : I), (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = ⨆ (p : I), (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c := rfl
      have h₄ : ⨆ (p : I), (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = ⊤ := by
        calc
          ⨆ (p : I), (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c = ⨆ (p : I), (p.val : PersistenceSubmodule M) c := rfl
          _ = ⨆ (p : (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))), p c := by
            simp [SetLike.coe]
            <;>
            congr <;>
            ext <;>
            simp [SetLike.coe]
            <;>
            aesop
          _ = ⊤ := by
            have h₅ : ⨆ (p : (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))), p c = ⊤ := by
              have h₆ : ⨆ (p : (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M))), p c = ⊤ := by
                -- Use the previously proven pointwise_sSup_eq_top lemma
                have h₇ : ⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c = ⊤ := by
                  have h₈ : ⨆ p ∈ (I : Set (PersistenceSubmodule M)), p c = ⊤ := by
                    simpa [iSup_iSup_eq_iSup_prod, sSup_def, iSup_eSubmodule, iSup_const] using pointwise_iSup_eq_top I c
                  exact h₈
                -- Convert the iSup over set to iSup over subtype
                simpa [iSup_iSup_eq_iSup_prod, sSup_def, iSup_eSubmodule, iSup_const] using h₇
              exact h₆
            simpa [iSup_iSup_eq_iSup_prod, sSup_def, iSup_eSubmodule, iSup_const] using h₅
      simpa [iSup_iSup_eq_iSup_prod, sSup_def, iSup_eSubmodule, iSup_const] using h₄
    simpa [iSup_iSup_eq_iSup_prod, sSup_def, iSup_eSubmodule, iSup_const] using h₂
